metadata.title: Unbunked — Fact-checking the news
metadata.description: Unbunked verifies the news claim by claim and assigns it a verdict, backed by sources.

common.tagline: Fact-checking the news, verified claim by claim

footer.tagline: Fact-checking the news, verified claim by claim.

verdicts.title: Our verdict scale
verdicts.subtitle: Every analysis is assigned one of these five verdicts.

reliable.label: Reliable
reliable.description: Solid, well-sourced and consistent with the facts.

nuanced.label: Nuanced
nuanced.description: Largely true, but incomplete or context-dependent.

fragile.label: Fragile
fragile.description: Fragile reliability: major reservations about the facts or sources.

criteria.sectionHint: The overall score is the weighted average of these criteria (each out of 100). A criterion that can't be assessed is not shown.

factuality.label: Factuality
factuality.description: Are the stated facts true and backed by the sources?

corroboration.label: Corroboration
corroboration.description: Are the central facts confirmed by independent external sources?

sourcing.label: Sourcing
sourcing.description: Are the cited sources solid, independent and verifiable?

completeness.label: Completeness
completeness.description: Are the important facts present rather than omitted by selection?

transparency.label: Transparency
transparency.description: Are the author, date, editorial owner and funding identifiable?

recency.label: Recency
recency.description: Is the information up to date rather than stale?

descriptors.caption: Descriptors — they describe the article but are not part of the score.

methodology.title: How Unbunked rates the news
methodology.intro: Every article goes through the same pipeline: we isolate the checkable claims, test them against sources via web search, then derive a verdict and scores. Here is what each one measures.
methodology.scoreTitle: The overall score and its criteria
methodology.scoreBody: The reliability score (0-100) shown prominently is computed by our code: it is the weighted average of the criteria, each rated out of 100, so it is reproducible and explainable. Reliability measures rigour, not orientation: an article can be engaged and reliable. When evidence is missing, the score becomes "—" (unverifiable) rather than an arbitrary number.
methodology.optionalHint: These criteria are only scored when they can be judged reliably; otherwise they are left out rather than producing an arbitrary number.

home.title: Unbunked's foundations are in place
home.body: Light/dark theme, internationalization and the verdict system are wired up. The article feed lands in the next step.

auth.loginSubtitle: The newsroom saved your seat. Ready to resume fact-checking?
auth.heroSubtitle: Submit a link, the pipeline extracts each claim, cross-checks it against sources, and publishes a verdict. You stay in control of every nuance.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Cookieless, privacy-friendly audience measurement.
analytics.noReferrers: No external sources for this range.

privacy.title: Privacy & retention
privacy.description: No cookies, no personal data. Unique visitors are counted with an anonymous identifier that rotates every day.
privacy.retention: Data is kept for at most {days} days.
privacy.oldest: Oldest data point: {date}. Maximum retention: {days} days.

costs.title: AI costs
costs.subtitle: Tokens consumed and estimated production cost per article.
costs.note: Costs estimated from Anthropic pricing, in US dollars (USD). Cache tokens (reads and writes) are billed at a reduced rate. Web search is billed separately from tokens (~$10 per 1000 requests) and is included in the costs above.

table.title: Per-article breakdown
table.truncated: Showing the {count} most recent articles; the totals above cover all of them.

submit.title: Analyze an article
submit.modelHint: Model used for verification, judgement and rewriting. Extraction stays on Haiku.

diagnostics.title: Run diagnostics
diagnostics.empty: No diagnostics recorded for this run.

jobs.title: Runs
jobs.subtitle: Every analysis run, newest first. Relaunch a failed or finished run.

review.title: Review before publishing
review.headline: Title
review.scoreHint: Default value, not computed — normally derived from the criteria.
review.criteriaHint: Uncheck an optional criterion if it can't be assessed reliably.
review.scrapeDebugEmpty: No scrape diagnostics for this article.
review.deletedBanner: This article is deleted: hidden from the public. Restore it or relaunch an analysis.

rewrites.title: Unbunked version (rewrite)
rewrites.hint: Markdown. Use [[claim:N]] to anchor a correction to claim N.

article.claimsBreakdownHint: Status of each checked claim in the article. Full detail below.
article.readingTitle: The article, checked paragraph by paragraph
article.readingIntroSubtitle: The original text on the left, Unbunked's verification on the right.

suggestions.title: On the same topic, better rated
suggestions.fallbackBody: We don't have a reliable article on this subject yet. Got a source worth checking? Submit it and we'll take care of the rest.

views.switchHint: Pick your view: the annotated original article, or Unbunked's rewrite.

unbunkedRewrite.intro: An Unbunked rewrite of the article in your language, with problematic passages corrected inline. The ① marks link to the matching fact-check.
unbunkedRewrite.fallbackNotice: Rewrite not available in your language; showing the default.

propose.title: Submit an article
propose.description: Got something to fact-check? Share the link and we'll handle the rest.
propose.success: Thank you! Your proposal has been received.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.intro: Unbunked measures its audience without ever tracking you: no cookies, no advertising profile, no data resold.

cookieless.title: No cookies, no consent banner
cookieless.body: Our analytics set no cookies and store nothing on your device. Under the GDPR and the ePrivacy directive, no consent banner is therefore required.

collected.title: What we measure
collected.body: For each page viewed we only record the page visited, the display language and the domain name of the site that may have referred you to us. The query string (anything after the “?” in the address) is never kept.

noPersonalData.title: No personal data
noPersonalData.body: Your IP address and browser are never stored. They are only used to compute an anonymous identifier that changes every day, letting us count unique visitors without ever being able to recognise you across days or re-identify you.

retention.title: Retention period
retention.body: Measurements are automatically purged after 90 days. We apply data minimisation: only aggregated, anonymous data is kept, for as long as strictly necessary.

rights.title: Your rights and Do Not Track
rights.body: If your browser sends the “Do Not Track” or “Global Privacy Control” signal, nothing is measured. Since no personal data is collected, there is nothing about you to access, rectify or erase.
